Name: geometric_subspace_diagnostics
Title: Geometric Subspace Diagnostics for Training-Free Mixed-Precision Optimization
Short Hypothesis: Geometric subspace diagnostics, particularly Hessian conditioning and gradient covariance decay, can effectively guide mixed-precision optimization without requiring additional training, leading to resource-efficient and mathematically transparent routing strategies.
Related Work: Existing work on mixed-precision optimization often relies on heuristic approaches or extensive training data. Recent advances in curvature-aware optimization and spectral quantization theory provide a foundation for deterministic diagnostics, but these have not been systematically applied to mixed-precision routing. This proposal distinguishes itself by leveraging training-free geometric subspace diagnostics to inform precision decisions, replacing heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii and explicit condition number constraints.
Abstract: Mixed-precision optimization holds promise for resource-efficient machine learning, but existing approaches often rely on heuristic strategies or extensive training data. We propose a training-free framework that leverages geometric subspace diagnostics—specifically Hessian conditioning and gradient covariance decay—to guide precision decisions. By quantifying feature entanglement and formalizing stability radii, our approach replaces heuristic fragility claims with deterministic optimization proxies. We operationalize this framework through a curvature-aware precision routing protocol that enforces explicit numerical stability analysis and strict computational overhead bounds. Empirical validation on single-GPU deployments demonstrates significant improvements in resource efficiency and mathematical transparency, with formal PAC-Bayes verification and systematic ablation protocols ensuring robustness. This work bridges foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints, offering a scalable and theoretically grounded solution for mixed-precision optimization.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Evaluate the effectiveness of Hessian conditioning and gradient covariance decay in guiding precision decisions. Use synthetic datasets with known curvature properties to validate the diagnostic accuracy.

Experiment 2: Implement the curvature-aware precision routing protocol on standard benchmark datasets (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet) and compare resource efficiency and accuracy against state-of-the-art mixed-precision methods.

Experiment 3: Conduct a systematic ablation study to isolate the impact of geometric subspace diagnostics on routing performance. Measure the variance in results across multiple seeds to ensure robustness.
Experiment 4: Formal PAC-Bayes verification to quantify generalization bounds under mixed-precision conditions. Use explicit condition number constraints to evaluate numerical stability.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The framework relies on accurate geometric subspace diagnostics, which may be challenging to compute for very high-dimensional datasets.
Deterministic optimization proxies may not capture all nuances of mixed-precision optimization, potentially limiting performance in some scenarios.
The approach is optimized for single-GPU deployments, and scalability to multi-GPU or distributed environments remains an open question.
